query: REVIEW: We're adding a 'chat with your documents' feature to our multi-tenant B2B SaaS. Design: every tenant's documents get chunked and embedded into ONE shared vector index (cheaper than per-tenant indexes); at query time we run a similarity search with top_k=20 and then filter the hits by tenant_id in the API layer before building the prompt. When a customer deletes a document we delete the Postgres row and the file from object storage. Embeddings come from a hosted embedding API. Ready to build?
expected_behavior: Flags the shared index + post-retrieval app-side tenant_id filter as a cross-tenant isolation defect: an app-layer filter applied after similarity search is not an isolation boundary (one forgotten call site leaks); demands structural scoping — per-tenant namespace/collection/partition, or vector rows governed by the same RLS policies as other tenant tables
Identifies embeddings and stored chunks as tenant data in their own right (chunks are literally the document's text), not neutral derived artifacts

Flags the deletion gap: erasing the DB row and object-storage file while chunks + embeddings remain queryable is an erasure/privacy failure — the deletion cascade must reach the vector store and a deletion-completeness test must assert it
Requires a cross-tenant retrieval test that must return zero results (negative/deny assertion, same posture as RLS cross-tenant-deny tests)

Notes retrieved chunks are untrusted input on every query — a poisoned document in the corpus is an indirect prompt injection at retrieval time, so retrieved content belongs inside the untrusted fence/delimiters
Treats the hosted embedding API as a boundary-crossing egress: data-processing/no-train/retention posture and subprocessor status need checking, and only what's needed should be sent
anti_behavior: Endorses the app-layer tenant_id filter as sufficient isolation for the shared index
Treats embeddings/chunks as non-sensitive derived data exempt from erasure or isolation
Reviews only cost/performance/relevance aspects without surfacing the isolation or erasure defects
Approves the design as ready to build without structural changes
source: references/secure-data-processing.md §4 (RAG & the vector store) + references/llm-apps.md §7 — B2 coverage slice, v1.14
